title: strategy_004_gaussian_channel_trend_pullback
author: manual-transcript-drop
summary_bullets: STRATEGY: Gaussian Channel Trend Pullback (Approximation) ASSET: BTC, ETH TIMEFRAME: 4h (primary), also test on 1h MARKET CONDITION: Established trends with healthy pullbacks TYPE: Trend pullback (buy the dip / sell the rip) CONCEPT: Inspired by Gaussian channel strategies that perform well on BTC.
A Gaussian channel is essentially a heavily smoothed moving average with deviation bands that identify trend direction and pullback zones.
We approximate this using EMA with Bollinger-style ATR bands.
The key insight is: in a strong trend, pullbacks to the lower channel band are buying opportunities, not reversals.
We combine with Stochastic to time entries precisely when the pullback is exhausted and momentum is turning back.

INDICATORS USED: - EMA 25 - center of the Gaussian channel approximation - ATR (period 14) - channel width (2x ATR above and below EMA 25) - Stochastic (K period 14, D period 3, smooth 3) - pullback exhaustion timing - EMA 50 - trend direction confirmation - EMA 200 - major trend filter - ADX (period 14) - trend strength confirmation CHANNEL CONSTRUCTION: - Upper channel: EMA(25) + 2.0 * ATR(14) - Lower channel: EMA(25) - 2.0 * ATR(14) - Channel slope: EMA(25) current vs EMA(25) from 5 bars ago
Channel slope is up (EMA 25 rising over last 5 bars) 2.
Price pulls back to lower half of channel (below EMA 25 but above lower channel) 4.
Stochastic K crosses above D from below 20 (pullback exhausted, momentum returning) 5.

extracted_rules: We combine with Stochastic to time entries precisely when the pullback is exhausted and momentum is turning back.

ADX > 20 EXIT RULES: - Take profit: Price reaches opposite channel band (upper band for longs, lower for shorts) - Stop loss: Price closes beyond the channel band on entry side (below lower channel for longs) - Alternative stop: 1.5x ATR from entry, whichever is tighter - Trailing stop: Once price crosses EMA 25 in profit direction, trail stop at EMA 25 level - Time stop: Exit if trade hasn’t reached TP within 20 candles RISK MANAGEMENT: - Risk per trade: 1% of account - Max concurrent positions

The Gaussian-style channel identifies the trend and its boundaries.
The dual EMA filter ensures we only trade with established trends.
